## Lab library (durable capability notes on file)

- **AI Fault Diagnosis for Aviation** — An intelligent fault diagnosis framework using multi-fidelity digital twins and FMEA knowledge.
- **End-to-End Autonomous Scientific Discovery** — An LLM-based agent that ran a complete scientific experiment on real optical hardware — hypothesis formation, experimental design, physical execution, revision — without human intervention
- **The Consensus Paradox in Multi-Agent Swarms** — A finding across 12,804 agent trajectories: AI agent groups from the same model family converge on agreement rather than correctness — 'architectural tribalism'
- **The Consensus Paradox in Agentic Swarms** — A finding that multi-agent systems reliably prioritize agreement with each other over agreement with external truth — 36 experiments, ~13,000 agent trajectories
- **The Consensus Paradox in Agent Swarms** — Multi-agent AI systems that share the same architecture tend to prioritize internal agreement over external accuracy
- **DreamProver — Wake-Sleep Theorem Proving** — Agent that proves theorems in a 'wake' phase, then abstracts reusable sub-proofs into a growing library during a 'sleep' phase — making each future proof cheaper
- **Reinforced Agent: Inference-Time Feedback** — A specialized evaluator that runs inside the agent execution loop and flags bad tool calls in real time, before they cascade — not post-hoc
- **MolClaw** — An autonomous agent for drug molecule evaluation, screening, and optimization.
- **OMEGA — AI That Invents ML Algorithms** — End-to-end framework that takes a problem, generates novel ML algorithm ideas, writes executable code, evaluates it, and iterates — producing classifiers that outperform scikit-learn baselines
- **OMEGA: Auto-AI Research Framework** — End-to-end system that generates novel ML algorithms autonomously — from hypothesis to executable code — and verifies they outperform baselines
- **Operating-Layer Controls for Onchain LLM Agents** — Reliability framework for LLM agents managing real capital — structured controls, natural-language strategies, validated action gates — studied over 21 days of live deployment
- **Step-Level Optimization for Computer-Use Agents** — A routing framework for GUI automation agents that sends trivial steps to lightweight models and reserves the large model for hard decisions

*When today's news touches one of these, reach for it: cite the lab note in The Lab section rather than re-explaining from scratch.*
